title: Bootstrap Stability Meta-Investigation
question: Does the reps=200 deep-validation breach signal reflect a real robustness problem or Monte Carlo instability in path bootstrap estimation?
interpretation: The reps=200 breaches are not evidence of a better nearby model; they are a low-resolution estimate on a thin-slack reference.
The canonical reference becomes breach-free by reps=300 and exactly reproduces the Phase28d promotion metrics by reps=500.
Current governance should treat reps<300 as exploratory only and require reps>=300, preferably reps=500, for winner-selection or promotion decisions in this neighborhood.
next_step: If further research continues, pivot to a genuinely new mechanism search rather than more local threshold nudges around v28d_sig02.
